objections.claim: The read-only oracle excludes `scripts/` without forbidding production modules or package entry points from importing those excluded files. A verifier can therefore statically import a write-capable helper under `scripts/` and pass the stated scan, so the oracle does not discriminate a straightforward violation of the READ-ONLY contract.

The plan-version closed shape does not define scalar types for `plan_version`, `superseded_by`, `note`, or `active_plan.version`. An object, number, empty string, or null can therefore satisfy an implementation that checks only the stated keys and selected fields, potentially yielding `consistent` for a malformed authority identity.

The fail-closed ingestion anomaly list omits malformed/unreadable manifest-discovered JSON and malformed manifest roots, while the oracle list does not explicitly exercise discovery end to end. An ingester that skips an unparseable retirement record or ignores a newly declared memory directory can consequently omit a retirement surface and still return the real-data golden `consistent`.
objections.scope: §§4–5 record discovery, fail-closed ingestion, and oracle coverage
